mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show a positive or neutral correlation with CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.4% threshold across multiple inflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll growth and unemployment decline signal predicts positive 20-day returns in fewer than 45% of historical expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output that directly increases corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates exhibit a Rank IC below 0.01 against 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates weakening manufacturing activity that reduces near-term corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below the 0.5% monthly decline threshold.
